title: Curaleaf Reports Q1 2024 Revenue Of $339M, 48% Gross Margin Amid Strategic Global Expansion
text: Curaleaf Holdings reported first quarter 2024 revenue of $339 million with a 48% gross margin. The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $76.7 million at a 23% margin, representing a 40 basis point improvement compared to the prior year. The company expanded its U.S. retail footprint to 145 stores, including its first adult-use location in New York's Hudson Valley. Product innovations included launching Stir by Zero Proof beverages in Illinois and Stiq vape cartridges in Arizona. Internationally, Curaleaf strengthened its position through acquiring Northern Green Canada for approximately $16 million plus earnout considerations, establishing EU-GMP certified production capacity. The company also completed its first wholesale shipment into the Czech Republic and acquired Can4Med, a Polish pharmaceutical wholesaler. Executive Chairman Boris Jordan characterized the quarter as an important inflection point, noting the company's evolution from an MSO (multi-state operator) to an MCO (multi-country operator) spanning 15+ countries globally.
